_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Post traumatic painful partial collapse in a non-osteoporotic vertebra without retropulsion causing cord compression

preauth.label: Clinical notes including clinical evaluation and confirming the diagnosis
Investigations (optional) Complete blood count, INR/PT, Serum creatinine

claim.label: In case of accident/trauma – FIR (if applicable)
Documentary evidence/Invoice of cement used as applicable

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history especially accident history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment available?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, investigations, or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Evidence of breach in posterior cortex of vertebral body, epidural extension, vertebra plana, or uncorrectable coagulopathy without justification.
Missing X-ray Spine (AP and lateral view) at pre-authorization.
Missing detailed procedure / operative notes at claim submission.
Missing documentary evidence/invoice of cement used (if applicable) at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at District/Tertiary Hospital (centers equipped with capability to perform Interventional neuroradiology)
clinical_key_pointers: Vertebroplasty is injection of bone cement into a diseased vertebra, Percutaneously, under image guidance. This is done to provide pain relief and to strengthen the diseased vertebra.
contraindications: Breach in the posterior cortex of the vertebral body
